question.text: Which of the following actions would NEGATIVELY impact a person's credit score?

answer.text: Paying all loan EMIs on time
Having a healthy mix of secured and unsecured credit
High utilization of credit card limits and frequent loan enquiries

explanation.text: **The Logic:** High credit card utilization relative to limits, too many unsecured loans, and frequent credit enquiries all negatively impact the credit score. On-time payments, credit mix diversity, and periodic review help maintain or improve the score. **The Trap:** Checking your own credit report does NOT hurt your score — only hard enquiries from lenders do. **Cross-Exam Context:** Credit score factors are tested in X-A (Ch 4). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tell clients to keep credit card utilization below 30% of their limit and avoid applying for multiple loans simultaneously.
